question: How can I apply for admission?
answer: You can apply online through our university admission portal. Visit the official website, create an account, fill in the application form, upload required documents, and submit the application fee. The online portal opens 3 months before the deadline.

question: What are the admission requirements?
answer: Admission requirements include: minimum 60% marks in previous degree, valid entrance test score, completed application form, academic transcripts, and English proficiency proof for international students. Some programs may have additional prerequisites.

question: What documents are required for admission?
answer: You need to submit: 1) Completed application form, 2) Academic transcripts (all previous degrees), 3) 2 letters of recommendation, 4) Statement of purpose, 5) Valid CNIC or passport copy, 6) Domicile certificate, 7) Recent passport-size photographs, and 8) Application fee receipt.

question: What is the admission deadline?
answer: The fall semester deadline is August 15. The spring semester deadline is January 10. Late applications may be accepted until August 30 and January 25 respectively, with a late fee of $25. We recommend applying early as seats fill quickly.

question: What is the fee structure?
answer: Undergraduate tuition is $12,000 per year for in-state and $24,000 for out-of-state students. Graduate programs range from $15,000 to $30,000 per year. Additional fees include library fee ($200), lab fee ($150 per course), and sports fee ($100) per semester.

question: What scholarships are available?
answer: We offer merit-based scholarships (up to 100% tuition for top 5% students), need-based financial aid, sports scholarships, and special scholarships for toppers from remote areas. You can apply for scholarships after receiving your admission offer.

question: What programs are offered?
answer: We offer undergraduate programs in Computer Science, Business Administration, Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Psychology, Biology, Mathematics, Economics, Law, and Architecture. Graduate programs include MBA, MS Data Science, MS Computer Science, MA Education, and PhD in various disciplines.

question: Do you offer BS Computer Science?
answer: Yes, we offer a 4-year BS Computer Science program. The curriculum covers programming, data structures, algorithms, artificial intelligence, databases, software engineering, and web development. Entry requires 60% marks in intermediate with Mathematics.

question: What is the eligibility criteria?
answer: Eligibility criteria: Minimum 60% marks in intermediate or equivalent, at least 50% in the entrance test, and no gap of more than 2 years after previous qualification. International students need equivalency certificate and English proficiency test scores.

question: Is there an entry test for admission?
answer: Yes, all applicants must pass the university entrance test. The test covers English, Mathematics, Analytical Reasoning, and General Knowledge. You can prepare using sample papers available on our website. The test fee is $20. Test results are valid for one year.

question: How is the merit list prepared?
answer: The merit list is based on 40% intermediate marks, 30% entrance test score, 20% interview performance, and 10% extracurricular achievements. Merit lists are published on the university website and notice board. The first merit list is usually announced 2 weeks after the test.

question: Is hostel accommodation available?
answer: Yes, we have separate hostels for male and female students with a capacity of 2,000 students. Rooms are shared (2-3 students per room). Facilities include WiFi, 24/7 electricity backup, mess, laundry, and common room. Hostel fee is $1,500 per semester including meals.

question: Is transport facility available?
answer: Yes, the university operates a fleet of buses covering major routes within a 30 km radius. Monthly transport fee is $80. Bus schedules are available on the university portal. Students can also use the campus shuttle service that operates between departments.

question: What is the admission office contact information?
answer: The admission office is located in the Admin Block, Ground Floor. Office hours: Monday to Friday, 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM. Phone: +1-555-0100, Email: admissions@university.edu. You can also visit the admission section on our website for live chat support.

question: How can I contact the university for support?
answer: For general inquiries: info@university.edu or +1-555-0101. For admission-specific queries: admissions@university.edu or +1-555-0100. For technical support: helpdesk@university.edu. Our support team responds within 24 hours during working days.

question: How can I check my application status?
answer: You can check your application status by logging into the admission portal using your application ID and password. The portal shows real-time updates including document verification status, test schedule, interview date, and final admission decision.

question: Is there an interview for admission?
answer: Yes, shortlisted candidates are called for an interview. The interview panel includes faculty members from the applied department. It assesses your subject knowledge, communication skills, and motivation. Interview schedules are shared via email and the admission portal.

question: Is there an orientation program for new students?
answer: Yes, the university organizes a one-week orientation program for all new students. It includes campus tours, introduction to faculty, library orientation, club sign-ups, and academic advising sessions. Orientation is held one week before classes begin.

question: What is the semester system?
answer: The university follows a semester system with two regular semesters per year: Fall (September to January) and Spring (February to June). Each semester is 18 weeks long including final exams. Summer sessions are also available for elective courses.

question: How many credit hours are required to graduate?
answer: For BS programs, you need 130-136 credit hours to graduate. Each course is typically 3 credit hours. A semester load is 15-18 credit hours. Minimum CGPA of 2.0 is required for graduation. You can take up to 6 years to complete a 4-year degree.

question: Can transfer students apply?
answer: Yes, transfer students from accredited institutions are welcome. You need to submit transcripts from all previous institutions for evaluation. A maximum of 60 credit hours can be transferred. A minimum CGPA of 2.5 in previous institution is required.

question: Can international students apply?
answer: Yes, international students are welcome. Additional requirements include: valid passport, student visa, English proficiency test (TOEFL 80+ or IELTS 6.5+), financial support proof, and equivalency certificate from IBCC. International tuition is $24,000 per year.

question: What is the refund policy?
answer: Full refund is available if you withdraw within the first week of classes. 75% refund in the second week, 50% in the third week, and no refund after the fourth week. The application fee and admission processing fee are non-refundable.

question: Is late admission possible?
answer: Late admission may be considered if seats remain unfilled after the regular admission process. A late fee of $25 applies. Late applicants are admitted on a first-come, first-served basis. Contact the admission office for late application availability.

question: What is the support email for admission queries?
answer: For admission-related queries, email us at admissions@university.edu. For technical issues with the online portal, email helpdesk@university.edu. For scholarship inquiries, email financialaid@university.edu. We aim to reply within 24 hours.
